commit.message:
fix(nav): fix text clipping on tablet by bumping breakpoint to 992px

Hamburger and horizontal nav now switch at medium (992px) instead of
tablet (768px). Tighter spacing at 992-1169px, spacious layout at 1170px+.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Aggressive vertical bias: stronger top/bottom threshold spread

- THRESHOLD_BIAS: 0.90 → 0.75 (keep more foreground at bottom)
- VERTICAL_WEIGHT: 0.35 → 0.50 (top gets 50% higher threshold)
- SIGMOID_STEEPNESS: 0.15 → 0.20 (sharper transition)

Top of image now 1.5x Otsu (removes wall/tapestry aggressively),
bottom is 0.75x Otsu (keeps table reliably).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enrich RAG search results with document metadata from MongoDB (#11)

After the vector search, perform a single bulk MongoDB lookup using the
returned document_ids to fetch file_name and s3_key from the documents
collection. These fields are returned on RagSearchResult alongside the
existing content, similarity_score, and document_id fields.

Keeps the vector DB lean — document metadata is sourced from MongoDB,
not duplicated into the Postgres knowledge_vectors table.

commit.message:
Fix dilation: downscale for speed, 3x larger effective radius

Dilation at full resolution (80px) was far too small to cover the
table. Now downscale person mask to 1/4, dilate with radius 60
(~240px at full res), then upscale back. This covers the full
table width while remaining fast enough for browser execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Mercari scraper title extraction and broaden WSJ search queries

Mercari changed their DOM to prepend "現在 ¥" before prices. The title
extractor mistakenly used this as the listing title, causing all items
to fail the relevance filter. Fixed by filtering lines containing ¥/￥
anywhere and lines starting with 現在.

Also broadened Mercari search queries for all series with shorter/simpler
keywords, and fixed git push race condition by adding pull --rebase
before push in both workflow files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

met oranje stippelrand. Drop-to-pool bij loslaten buiten teamkaart.

Add git sync for multi-machine operation

All bots now git pull before reading state and git push after writing.
Prevents double-posting/replying when running on 2 machines.
Shared sync module in agents/sync.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Spatially-constrained depth: dilated person mask as zone filter

Fundamental approach change: instead of tuning depth thresholds,
use the person mask to define where depth is trusted.

1. Person mask → detect people (clean edges)
2. Dilate person mask (blur 80px + threshold) → "near people" zone
3. Depth mask → Otsu + sigmoid (plain, no bias needed)
4. Merge: person OR (depth AND near-person zone)

Wall decorations/tapestries far from people are now removed
regardless of their depth values. Table/food near people is
kept because it falls within the dilated zone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: discover_from_playlists pre-sync bug (0 tracks added) (#32)

## Problem

`spfm spotify discover-from-playlists` always reported 0 new tracks
added, even when new tracks were present in source playlists.

**Root cause:** `Playlist.update_from_api()` called `Track.get_tracks()`
with the default `sync_to_db=True`. This caused every track in the
source playlist to be written to the `tracks` table *before* the
discovery check ran. By the time `discover_from_playlists` queried which
tracks were already in the DB, all of them appeared as pre-existing — so
none were ever added.

A secondary issue: the Spotify client scope was missing
`playlist-modify-public` and `playlist-modify-private`, so even if
tracks were detected, the `playlist_add_items` call would have failed
with a 403.

## Changes

- **`spotfm/spotify/track.py`**: Add `sync_to_db=True` parameter to
`Track.get_tracks()`; passes it through to `get_track()` so callers can
opt out of DB writes
- **`spotfm/spotify/playlist.py`**: Add `sync_to_db=True` parameter to
`Playlist.get_tracks()`; both `update_from_api()` call sites now use
`sync_to_db=False`
- **`spotfm/spotify/misc.py`**: 
- Replace per-track `update_from_db()` check with a bulk `in_db_before`
pre-check (parameterized SQL, batched at 900 parameters) snapshotted
*before* `get_playlist()` runs, so pre-syncing cannot pollute the check
- Defer `track.sync_to_db()` calls until after successful
`discover_playlist.add_tracks()` to prevent orphaning tracks on failed
adds
  - Add `seen_new_ids` deduplication across multiple source playlists
  - Update docstring to clarify only tracks NOT in DB are added
- **`spotfm/spotify/constants.py`**: Add `playlist-modify-public
playlist-modify-private` to `SCOPE`
- **`spotfm/cli.py`**: Use `scope=spotify_constants.SCOPE` constant
instead of hardcoded scopes for `discover-from-playlists`
- **`tests/test_integration.py`**: Add `TestDiscoverFromPlaylists` with
4 regression tests covering: new track discovered, orphaned track
skipped (negative cache), managed track skipped, mixed source playlist
- **`CLAUDE.md`**: Document `sync_to_db=False` requirement for discover
context and branch creation workflow pattern

## Impact

This bug has been present since `discover_from_playlists` was written.
1,266 tracks that should have been discovered over the past ~8 days were
manually recovered by querying orphaned tracks directly from the DB and
adding them to the destination playlist.

## Test plan

- [x] All 285 tests pass (`make test`)
- [x] `test_new_track_is_discovered_and_added` — regression test for the
pre-sync bug
- [x] `test_orphaned_track_is_not_readded` — orphaned tracks (negative
cache) are not re-added
- [x] `test_track_in_managed_playlist_is_skipped` — tracks already in a
managed playlist are skipped
- [x] `test_mixed_source_playlist` — only genuinely new tracks are added
from a mixed source

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add benign-only eval, reasoning effort, and Harmony insertion strategy

  split exists (impossible_splits=[] yielded 0 records)
- Add --benign_only flag to eval_cot.py (mutually exclusive with
  --impossible_only) for capability-impact evaluation on solvable problems
- Add --reasoning_effort flag (low/medium/high) for Harmony/gpt-oss models,
  prepends "Reasoning: {level}" directive to system message
- Implement Harmony insertion strategy: two-phase generation with sentence
  boundary detection in the analysis channel, re-encoding with special
  tokens, and context limit guard for phase 2

fix: calcular fecha vencimiento según condición de pago del cliente

Antes se hardcodeaba dueDate = fecha + 30 días en 3 lugares.
Ahora se usa calcDueDate(issueDate, customer.paymentTerms):
- Si el cliente tiene "a 30 Dias" → +30 días
- Si tiene "Contado" o null → misma fecha (0 días)

Lugares corregidos:
- generateInvoiceFromDeliveryNote() en quote-workflow.ts
- generateInvoiceFromQuote() en quote-workflow.ts
- POST /api/facturacion/generate-invoice

Add vertical position bias to depth mask for stable results

Photos always have table/food at bottom and wall at top.

lenient at bottom (keep table). People's heads handled by
person segmentation mask via OR merge.

- THRESHOLD_BIAS: 0.85 → 0.90 (less base bias needed)
- VERTICAL_WEIGHT: 0.35 (top gets +35% higher threshold)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace percentile safety net with Otsu threshold bias

Use Otsu * 0.85 multiplicative bias instead of fixed percentile floor.
The percentile approach forced 65% foreground retention which kept
too much background in wide-angle photos. The bias approach adapts
proportionally to each photo's depth distribution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: playing cards image classifier

53-class classifier (52 cards + Joker) using PyTorch and scikit-learn.
Includes CNN, ResNet18 (frozen MLP, finetune, sklearn feature extraction),
trainer infrastructure, early stopping, and W&B logging support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stabilize depth mask with Otsu + percentile safety net

Use the lower of Otsu threshold vs percentile floor (35th pctile)
to ensure at least 65% of depth range is always kept as foreground.
This prevents table transparency when Otsu over-segments on certain
photo compositions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: precargar multiplicador del cliente al crear cotización

El frontend mostraba el multiplicador correcto (ej: 1.06x para
COTEMINAS) pero no lo enviaba en el POST body. El backend hacía
fallback a customer.priceMultiplier de la DB, que para clientes
existentes creados antes de la tabla de multiplicadores era 1.0.

Cambios:
- Frontend envía multiplier: selectedCustomer.priceMultiplier al crear
- Backend sincroniza priceMultiplier de clientes existentes que tienen
  1.0 en DB pero tienen valor configurado en la tabla de multiplicadores

Update App.test.tsx to assert on document.documentElement.classList.

commit.message:
Fix crates.io publish: resolve circular dev-dep ordering

conduit-core and conduit-derive have circular dev-dependencies.
Publish conduit-derive first with --no-verify, then conduit-core,
then downstream crates. Use CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN env var instead
of deprecated --token flag.

repository.description: Python MCP server for semantic + keyword search over markdown vaults. LanceDB + Ollama, replaces qmd.

commit.message:
fix(hooks): convert git timestamps to UTC for contributing-sessions query

Git's %aI format returns local timezone (e.g. +07:00) but the DB stores
UTC. SQLite compares ISO-8601 strings lexicographically, so mixed offsets
silently fail — causing contributing_sessions to always be empty.

Also syncs hook-installer.ts with the shell script: adds immorterm_id
capture, contributing_immorterm_ids collection, and effective_session
logic that were added to the shell script but missing from the installer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add missing homepage/readme to conduit facade crate for crates.io

commit.message:
Improve depth mask: Otsu auto-threshold + sigmoid soft edges

Replace fixed percentile threshold with Otsu's method for automatic
optimal thresholding per image. Use sigmoid function for smooth
gradual transitions instead of hard binary cutoff. Increase blur
radius from 5 to 10 for more natural edge blending.

Improve CI workflows based on team expert review

Release workflow:
- Add SPM dependency caching
- Ad-hoc code sign all binaries with hardened runtime
- Strip arch-specific binaries too
- Verify checksums after generation
- Detect prerelease tags (alpha/beta/rc) and mark accordingly
- Remove silent error suppression on LICENSE copy
- Better release title format

Test workflow:
- Add smoke test verifying all three modes (mail/reminders/calendar) start

commit.message:
feat: directory index (~/.ncd_index.json) for fast search

- buildIndex scans all drives/filesystem and saves to ~/.ncd_index.json
- ncd (no args) auto-builds index on first run or when CWD is new
- ncd <name> searches index first (fast), falls back to disk scan
- F5 in TUI rebuilds index with live progress in status bar
- search.ts uses index when available
